prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that
TF's one line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed
UNASSIGNED units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept).
To rebuild membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED)
and FORM anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED
units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. A BUILD line is optional per
city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build (changing
discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: landed t56, six armies ashore;
      t57: capture attempted — failed, #3 lost; the enemy army holds (11,1);
      t58: enemy destroyer sighted at (9,4), near my empty transports

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-q = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X Y = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . h O i . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . j b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E . ?
 r3  . . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f g ?
 r4  . . . l . . ~ ~ ~ Y ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #4   army       (10,1)                       TF-5
  d  #5   army       (10,2)                       TF-5
  e  #6   army       (10,3)                       TF-5
  f  #7   army       (11,3)                       TF-5
  g  #8   army       (12,3)                       TF-5
  h  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  i  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  l  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  m  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  n  #19  army       (4,3) in city, NEW t57       UNASSIGNED
  o  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5
  p  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5
  q  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 2 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 26 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 4 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 23 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,2)
  X  enemy army garrisoning city (11,1)
  Y  enemy destroyer at (9,4), sighted t58, near my empty transports

It is TURN 58. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS m | solidify capital defense
TF-5: REINFORCE UNITS h i j n | increase mass for capture roll at (11,1)
message.reasoning_content:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** Strategic commander ("FARSHORE").
    *   **Constraints:** Output *ONLY* lines in specific forms (TF <id>: ... or FORM/REINFORCE/DISBAND/BUILD). No prose, no headers.
    *   **Goal:** Maintain strategic oversight. Don't move individual units explicitly; assign objectives via Task Forces (TFs).
    *   **Current Turn:** 58.
    *   **Status:**
        *   TF-2: Defend (2,0). Has armies #1, #2 in/around city (2,0) (actually #1 is in city, #2 at (2,1)). No losses yet. Capital secured.
        *   TF-5: Captured (11,1) mission. Arrived earlier. Landed six armies (#4-#7, wait, map shows 6 units c,g,h... no h,i,j,k,l,m,n? Let's check).
            *   Map units in TF-5: c (#4), d (#5), e (#6), f (#7), g (#8) are #4-#8. That's 5 armies. Plus n (#19) "army (4,3) in city NEW t57"? Wait.

            *   Map Units for TF-5: c (#4), d (#5), e (#6), f (#7), g (#8) at (10-12, 1-3). Plus o (#9 transport), p (#16 transport), q (#10 destroyer) at (9,1-3).
            *   Wait, map shows `h` (#11) and `i` (#12) as UNASSIGNED. `k` (#15) in city (4,3) UNASSIGNED. `l` (#17) UNASSIGNED. `m` (#18) in city (2,0) UNASSIGNED. `n` (#19) in city (4,3) NEW t57. This is confusing.
            *   Let's trust the "UNITS" section description over my quick scan of map markers for TF-5 membership. The report says TF-5 landed six armies ashore. It lost #3. Current TF-5 members listed in UNITS: c (#4), d (#5), e (#6), f (#7), g (#8) plus `n` (#19)? No, `n` is marked UNASSIGNED in the list below it.
            *   Wait, look at the `UNITS` list carefully:
                *   c #4 army (10,1) TF-5
                *   d #5 army (10,2) TF-5
                *   e #6 army (10,3) TF-5
                *   f #7 army (11,3) TF-5
                *   g #8 army (12,3) TF-5
                *   n #19 army (4,3) in city UNASSIGNED. Why is `n` marked UNASSIGNED?
                *   o #9 transport (9,1) TF-5
                *   p #16 transport (9,2) TF-5
                *   q #10 destroyer (9,3) TF-5
            *   Report says: "enemy army garrisoning city (11,2)" and "X enemy army garrisoning city (11,1)". The unit at (11,1) is likely the enemy.
            *   Current Objective: CAPTURE (11,1). Status: Attempt failed t57. Enemy holds. Enemy destroyer sighted at (9,4), near empty transports.
        *   UNASSIGNED Units: h (#11) army (1,0), i (#12) army (3,0), j (#13) army (1,1), k (#15) army in city (4,3), l (#17) army (3,4), m (#18) army in city (2,0) [Wait, TF-2 says `a` #1 is in city. Map has `m` #18 in city (2,0). Who owns the capital?]
            *   Capital: (2,0). TF-2 defends (2,0). Units in city/around: a (#1) army in city, b (#2) army at (2,1). Map shows `m` #18 also at (2,0) UNASSIGNED? This might be an overflow or spare garrison.
            *   City under construction: (4,3) building ARMY. Unit `k` (#15) is in there. Unit `n` (#19) is in there NEW t57 but marked UNASSIGNED. This looks like a copy-paste error in the prompt's state or `n` belongs to TF-5 logically despite being listed unassigned? Actually, look at `k` and `n`. Both at (4,3). City is building Transport (23 turns left) [Wait, city list says (4,1) building TRANSPORT. (4,3) building ARMY].
            *   Okay, focus on actionable orders.

    *   **Constraints Checklist:**
        1.  NO PROSE.
        2.  ONLY ORDER LINE FORMS.
        3.  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>.
        4.  Form new Task Forces if needed (FORM).
        5.  Reinforce/Task/Rebuild (REINFORCE, RETASK, DISBAND).
        6.  BUILD lines for cities (optional).

    *   **Strategic Situation:**
        *   TF-2 (Capital): Hold firm. No contact. Defend. Keep garrisoned status.
        *   TF-5 (East): Capture attempt at (11,1) failed. Enemy destroyer at (9,4) threatens empty transports (9,1/9,2). Transport is a target (Str 0) if not escorted or if in range of enemy destroyer. The destroyer is at (9,4), transports at (9,1)/(9,2). That's close. Destroyer Str = 2. Empty transports Str = 0. Transports would be sunk easily.
        *   Unassigned Units: Need a new Task Force to capture the city or secure the supply line. Currently TF-5 is stuck trying to take (11,1). They have land armies there. But they need more ammo/air support or they can't take it. Wait, rule says "A transport loads up to 6 armies at a city...". TF-5 has 5 armies currently on board land in map? No, map shows c-g (5 units) and o,p,q ships.

        *   The objective for TF-5 is "strike east and take enemy cities". Now stuck at (11,1) holding enemy army. Needs to either break through or call in reinforcements if it can't take it alone. But TF-2 is the capital (defend).
        *   There are Unassigned units on the West coast/edges: h(1,0), i(3,0), j(1,1), k(4,3) [in city?], l(3,4), m(2,0) [in city].
            *   City (2,0) is capital. TF-2 has a and b. m (#18) is listed in UNITS as "UNASSIGNED". This might be an extra garrison unit or the prompt implies TF-2 is the group name but units are unassigned? No, rule says TFs carry orders. TF-2 has "keep the capital garrisoned". It should hold the city. The prompt says `a` #1 is (2,0) in city. TF-2 includes `a` and `b`.
            *   Units `h`, `i`, `j`, `l` are free. Need to use them to help TF-5 or secure flanks.
        *   Crucial issue: Enemy Destroyer at (9,4). My transports at (9,1)/(9,2) are near it. If I keep the transport in TF-5 but the destroyer attacks it from adjacent water/land edge? (9,3 is my destroyer q). `q` #10 destroyer (9,3) escorting TF-5.
            *   Wait, map shows `q` at (9,3) and Transports at (9,1)/(9,2). The enemy destroyer is at (9,4). They are in line: Transport->Destroyer->Enemy Destroyer. If enemy DD shoots through or attacks transports? No, land/sea mix.
            *   Sea units move on water. Land/Sea meet at cities. Transports move on sea to land.
            *   Enemy destroyer is likely trying to attack the transports to prevent reloading/moving armies ashore.
        *   Strategy: TF-5 needs to secure (11,1). They lost #3 in the capture attempt earlier. They have 4 armies left there? Map shows c (#4) at (10,1), d (#5) at (10,2), e (#6) at (10,3), f (#7) at (11,3), g (#8) at (12,3). Wait, map markers are confusing.
            *   TF-5 report says "six armies ashore... #3 lost". Map shows `c` to `g`. That is 5 units. Where is the 6th? Maybe `n` (#19) which is marked UNASSIGNED but in city (4,3). This seems inconsistent. I must follow the current state.
            *   TF-5 needs more support or a push. But I cannot micromanage unit movement directly.
            *   I can REINFORCE TF-5 with unassigned units. But they are on land (1,0), (3,0), etc. To move them to the fight zone, they need transports.
            *   Transports (o, p) at (9,1/2) are vulnerable to Enemy DD (q enemy? No q is my destroyer). Wait, "q #10 destroyer... escorting". This is MY destroyer at (9,3). Enemy DD at (9,4).
            *   So I have a destroyer protecting the transports. But transport is Str 0, Destroyer is Str 2. Enemy DD Str 2. Combat Probabilistic. It's tight but I have protection for now.
            *   The bigger issue: Capture failed. TF-5 is bogged down.
            *   Unassigned armies (h, i, j) need to move. They are on the western flank.
            *   Action Plan:
                1.  Maintain Capital (TF-2).
                2.  Keep TF-5 trying to break out/capture (11,1), but protect their supply line (Transport/Destroyer) or reinforce them.
                3.  Assign Unassigned units to help capture (11,1). I can REINFORCE TF-5 with h,i,j? Or RETASK a TF?
                4.  I should probably FORM a new TF for reinforcement since unassigned are on the west and need to cross water or move quickly. But wait, Unassigned `k` (#15) is in City (4,3). City (4,3) builds ARMY.
                5.  Unassigned `l` (#17) at (3,4).
                6.  Most critical: TF-5 is currently vulnerable at the transports. I should order TF-5 to CONTINUE or RETASK? The goal is Capture (11,1). Since it failed, maybe they need to bring in fresh troops.
                7.  I need to issue orders for Unassigned units to get into a position. But I can only issue via REINFORCE or FORM or BUILD.
                8.  If I want `h`, `i`, `j` to help TF-5, I cannot just order them. I must add them to an existing TF (REINFORCE) or Form a new one and assign the target.
                9.  However, `h`, `i`, `j` are on land. They need a Transport to go East to join the fight at (11,1). But Transports are in TF-5 now. If I give them to TF-5, that's what they do.
                10. Wait, `q` is destroyer escorting TF-5's transports. `o`, `p` are transports. They are empty. TF-5 needs more transport capacity or can load armies there? Rule: "An army can never enter water. Transports carry them...".
                11. Actually, I cannot just move units without a TF ordering the objective. If I use REINFORCE `h,i,j` -> TF-5, they join the group. TF-5 is tasked with CAPTURE (11,1). The logic of the game says "If a task force contains armies and a transport and you give it an objective across water...".
                12. So if I add `h,i,j` to TF-5, they are now part of the group. But they are on land (West side). They need to load onto transports first. Transports are at East side (9,1/2) carrying armies? No, map says empty.
                13. If I Reinforce TF-5 with unassigned units, do they automatically move to the water point? The rule says: "YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS... you only give it an objective". It implies the *existing* logic of the unit movement is handled by subordinate officers unless I form a new group specifically.
                14. Actually, simpler logic: If Unassigned units are at (1,0) and TF-5 captures (11,1), can I order TF-2 to help? No, TF-2 is capital defense.
                15. Let's look at "CURRENT TASKINGS".
                    *   TF-5 status: Failed capture t57. Enemy holds.
                    *   Orders to give: Retask? Reinforce? Continue?
                    *   If they failed once, maybe they should RETASK to "SCOUT" the area or "REINFORCE" with more units. But I can't tell them how to move.
                    *   Given the Enemy DD is threatening my transports (which are empty), and TF-5 needs more mass to capture the city after a failed roll, I should add Unassigned units to TF-5 to boost their offensive capability. Wait, but unassigned are on land. If they join TF-5, do they go with it?
                    *   Better approach: Use `h`, `i`, `j` to build an escort or reinforce the transport group. Or, since I can't micromanage transport movement logic (it's handled by officers), I will simply order TF-5 to RETASK a different strategy or bring more units.
                    *   Actually, checking rules: "TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target>". I can change objective. Maybe they should focus on securing the transports first? But that takes too long.
                    *   Wait, looking at Unassigned units `h`, `i`, `j`. They are Armies. If I form a new TF from them or reinforce TF-5...
                    *   Let's check "UNITS" again. `o` and `p` (Transports) are in TF-5. `q` (Destroyer) is in TF-5. If I Reinforce TF-5 with unassigned armies, they are now under TF-5 command. They must march to a water point?
                    *   Wait, the rule: "An army can never enter water. Transports carry them... Your Officers do all of this." This implies if I order a TF that has Armies and Transports to go East, they move the transport with armies. If I Reinforce unassigned armies into TF-5 which is already holding an objective at East, I need to make sure they can load.
                    *   Maybe I should use `h`, `i`, `j` to defend or expand TF-2?
                    *   Let's stick to the main front. TF-5 failed capture. Need more manpower. Reinforce with h,i,j. Order them to move with the transport. Wait, if they are on land West, and Transports are East, how do they get there? The officers handle "marching armies to the transport". So I just add them to the group.
                    *   What about `k` (#15)? In City (4,3). It is UNASSIGNED. Should it go to TF-5 to support the capture? Or build a new unit in its city? It's an army inside a city. A city builds ONE unit at a time. The city at (4,3) is building ARMY. If I change production to TRANSPORT, I lose current progress.
                    *   Best move: Reinforce TF-5 with available unassigned armies. This adds combat power for the next capture roll.
                    *   Also, Capital TF-2 should stand firm (Continue).
                    *   Build order? City (4,1) building Transport. It's good for future logistics. Leave as is.
                    *   One thing: The Enemy Destroyer at (9,4) is a threat to Transports at (9,1)/(9,2). If I Reinforce TF-5, they need to be able to load onto the transports if the current ones are full or if more are needed.
                    *   Wait, map shows `h`, `i`, `j` are UNASSIGNED. `k` is in city (4,3). `l` is at (3,4). `m` is at (2,0).
                    *   I will issue:
                        1.  TF-2: CONTINUE (Capital security).
                        2.  TF-5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,1) | "increase offensive weight" or REINFORCE? The prompt says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE line IS that TF's one line." So I can't do "Retask AND Reinforce". I must choose one line per active TF.
                        3.  If I Reinforce TF-5, I say `REINFORCE UNITS <ids>`.
                        4.  If I RETASK TF-5, I say `RETASK CAPTURE...`. But the objective is already "CAPTURE (11,1)".
                        5.  Since it's Turn 58 and capture failed last turn, maybe Retask to SCOUT? Or keep Continue with Reinforce implied? No, explicit order needed.
                        6.  The most logical action: The goal is Capture. Reinforcing adds power for that attempt. So `REINFORCE UNITS h i j`.
                        7.  What about unassigned units on land? They need to load onto transports eventually. By reinforcing, they become part of the task force pushing toward (11,1).
                        8.  Wait, if I add them, I don't explicitly tell them how to get from West to East Transports. But the rule says officers do "march armies to transport... sail... unload". If I reinforce, they are added to the group tasked at (11,1).
                        9.  What about TF-2? It needs to continue defending. `CONTINUE`.
                        10. Are there any other actions? Maybe build a transport in another city? (4,1) building Transport is set for 23 turns. (1,2) building Transport 26 turns. No urgent need to change production without losing progress.
                        11. Wait, `m` (#18) is UNASSIGNED at (2,0). TF-2 has `a` (#1) and `b` (#2). Should I assign `m` to TF-2? It's in the same city. Reinforce TF-2 with `m`.
                        12. Yes, better security for Capital.
                        13. Wait, is it allowed to Reinforce a TF that is already defending? "REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED units to it." Yes.
                        14. So:
                            *   TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS m | (Optional) But maybe just continue?
                            *   TF-5: REINFORCE UNITS h i j.
                            *   This seems efficient. However, I need to ensure I don't violate "Every standing TF gets exactly one line".
                            *   TFs are: TF-2 and TF-5.
                            *   I must order every TF? The prompt says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that TF's one line." This implies if I don't list a TF, it has no order? "Output ONLY lines in these forms... Every standing TF gets exactly one line". This is a strict constraint. I must issue an order for *every* existing TF to ensure compliance with the system expectation (though usually this means at least one per active unit). If I don't mention TF-2, is it safe? To be safe and explicit, I will include both.
                            *   Actually, "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". So if there are 2 TFS, I need 2 lines.
                            *   What about DISBAND FORM? If I want to change membership.
                            *   Plan:
                                *   TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS m (Add garrison).
                                *   TF-5: REINFORCE UNITS h i j (Send reinforcements to capture attempt).
                                *   Wait, is `m` an army or a transport? "m #18 army (2,0) in city". Yes.
                                *   Is `k` (#15) part of the game logic? It's inside (4,3) UNASSIGNED. If I don't assign it, who owns it? The prompt says UNITS list is "your ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else". So I must use or disband all units not assigned to a TF. Currently `h,i,j,k,l,m,n` are unassigned (wait, n is #19). Wait, `n` (#19) is at (4,3) in city UNASSIGNED.
                                *   Wait, if `k` and `n` are inside the same city, one might be extra or one is a new arrival. The city is building ARMY. If I order `REINFORCE`, do they join TFs? Yes.
                                *   Strategy Check: Capital is secure but garrisoning could use more troops if enemy moves there.
                                *   East front is the main conflict. Needs mass.
                                *   Wait, `TF-2` already has `a` and `b`. `m` is another army in the city. Adding `m` increases local defense strength (even though capture rule says "A city can take several armies to secure"). But if enemy comes, I need more troops defending TF-2's territory.
                                *   So:
                                    1.  TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS m | Secure capital defenses
                                    2.  TF-5: REINFORCE UNITS h i j | Prepare for next assault on (11,1)
                                *   Wait, `TF-5` objective is "CAPTURE". Reinforcing them keeps that objective and adds power. This fits the rule "REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective".
                                *   I must ensure no prose.

2.  **Verify Constraints:**
    *   NO PROSE/HEADERS.
